name: Hallucination as primary safety concern
description: Hallucination — defined by participants as model assertions not grounded in provided context or factual reality — was identified as the most significant and most difficult-to-detect quality failure mode across all sectors.
key_evidence: RAG attribution failure (citing non-existent sources) most common form (INT-01, INT-03, INT-08, INT-13)
Confident confabulation particularly dangerous in medical/legal contexts where users trust apparent certainty (INT-02, INT-08, INT-14)
Context boundary violations: model uses training knowledge instead of provided document (INT-03, INT-09)
significance_for_qalis: Highest excerpt count (n=52). Most salient theme across medical (INT-02, INT-08, INT-14) and legal (INT-03, INT-09) participants. Directly motivates SF dimension (SF-1, SF-3).
representative_quotes: [Anonymised quote from a participant whose excerpt was coded C01]
